title: oxalis pulchella var. beneprotecta
intro: Oxalis pulchella var. beneprotecta, also known as purple wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the oxalis genus. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and astringent properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Oxalis pulchella var. beneprotecta can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it has also been used in traditional herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
